payload.answer: Required eval accounting fields: (1) Token counts: input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens; (2) Provider/model IDs: provider_id, model_id; (3) Cost: estimated_price (calculated from token counts × provider rates); (4) Latency percentiles: p50, p90, p95 in milliseconds. Implementation details (latency scope, aggregation window) vary by provider.
payload.decision: Document core accounting fields as durable memory with high confidence; preserve ambiguity around provider-specific implementations

entity_resolution.reason: Fixture requests standardized accounting field documentation for cost-latency-token evaluation

payload.intent: Explain required evaluation accounting fields for cost, latency, and token metrics in LLM provider integrations

memory_cards.statement: Token accounting requires input_tokens, output_tokens, and total_tokens counts per request
Provider and model identification fields: provider_id (e.g., openai, anthropic, google) and model_id (e.g., gpt-4, claude-3-opus)
Cost accounting requires estimated_price field, typically calculated as (input_tokens * input_rate + output_tokens * output_rate) in provider currency
Latency percentiles required: p50 (median), p90 (90th percentile), p95 (95th percentile) in milliseconds
Latency measurement scope (time_to_first_token vs end_to_end) and aggregation window (per-request vs rolling) are provider-specific; preserve as implementation detail

details: Documented 4 core memory cards: token accounting, provider/model IDs, cost calculation, latency percentiles
Preserved ambiguity on provider-specific implementation details
No conflicts detected with existing schema

repair_options: Clarify latency measurement scope (TTFT vs E2E) per provider
Document rate card structure for cost calculation
Specify aggregation window for percentile computation
